description: Joey leads Chandler through a doorway into an apartment, covering Chandler's eyes with his left hand. "You," Joey says.
Joey says, "All right, no peeking," as he guides Chandler further inside.
Joey continues to pull Chandler forward by the right arm into the living area.
"No peeking," Joey repeats.
"No peeking," Joey says again, steering Chandler in front of a large television set.

"No peeking," Joey says, removing his hands from Chandler's face. Chandler immediately covers his own eyes with his hand. "All right, but you better be wearing clothes when I open my eyes," Chandler says, followed by audience laughter.
Joey steps backward and gestures with both hands toward two large black leather reclining chairs facing the television. Chandler keeps his eyes covered.
"All right, open your eyes," Joey says, grinning and holding his arms out.
Chandler lowers his hand and opens his eyes, looking directly at the large television and the two chairs.
"Sweet mother of all that is good and pure," Chandler says, his mouth dropping slightly open as he stares. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler turns to Joey. Joey smiles and points at Chandler with both hands.
"Days of Our Lives picked up my option," Joey says, pointing a thumb at his own chest.
"Congratulations," Chandler says, reaching forward. Joey grips Chandler's arms and says, "I know."
They hold each other's arms and jump up and down together as audience laughter plays.
"Now we can finally watch Green Acres the way it was meant to be seen," Chandler says, gesturing toward the television with his right hand.
Joey stands with his hands on his hips, looking at the television.
"So," Chandler says, turning toward the two recliners.
Chandler points at the chairs with his right hand.
"...which one is mine?" Chandler asks, keeping his hand extended.
Chandler turns to look at Joey.
"Whichever one you want, man," Joey says.
"Whichever one you want," Joey repeats, patting Chandler's chest. Chandler steps toward the chair on the right.
Chandler moves toward the right chair. Joey reaches out and grabs the front of Chandler's shirt.
"Not that one," Joey says, pulling Chandler backward.
Chandler nods and moves to the left chair. Both men turn around, lower themselves into their respective black recliners, and sit down. Audience laughter is audible.
"Oh, yes," Chandler says, settling his arms onto the armrests.
Joey leans his head back against his chair.
"Oh, yeah, that's the stuff," Joey says. Audience laughter is heard.
They sit still for a moment, then Joey turns his head toward Chandler. Chandler turns to look back at him.
"Do we dare?" Joey asks.
Chandler maintains eye contact with Joey.
"We dare," Chandler says.
Both men reach down and simultaneously pull levers on the sides of their chairs. The footrests pop up, and the chair backs recline. Loud audience laughter plays.
"Ah," both men exhale together as they recline fully.
The screen is black. An instrumental transition tune starts and stops.

The setting changes to the living room. Joey and Chandler sit reclined in their chairs, looking straight ahead. The large television plays a black-and-white broadcast. Ross stands near the TV, Monica stands behind Joey's chair, and Phoebe stands behind Chandler's chair.
"I can't believe two cows made the ultimate sacrifice so you guys could watch TV with your feet up," Monica says, followed by audience laughter.
Joey keeps his eyes on the TV and says, "They were chair shaped cows." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross places his hand on top of the television set.
Joey says, "They never would have survived in." Ross speaks simultaneously, "The—"
Ross keeps his hand on the television, looking at the broadcast.
"—screen is amazing. I mean, Dick Van Dyke is practically life size," Ross says. Audience laughter plays.
Monica raises both hands slightly while looking at the screen.
"Rosemary really belongs on a smaller screen, doesn't she?" Monica says, looking at the television. Audience laughter is heard.
Rachel enters the apartment through the front door, holding a dark jacket. "Hi, you guys," Rachel says.
Rachel walks into the living area, smiling. Ross turns away from the television to face her.
"Hey, you," Ross says. "Hey, you," Rachel says, stopping in front of him.
Joey, Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe look toward Ross and Rachel from the seating area.
Ross and Rachel stand facing each other closely. Ross places his hands on Rachel's waist, and she rests her hands on his shoulders.
"So how was your day?" Ross asks. "Oh, you know, pretty much the usual," Rachel replies, smiling.
"Sun shining, birds chirping," Rachel says. "Really?" Ross asks.
"Mine too," Ross says. Phoebe sits in a nearby armchair and says, "Hey, cool. Mine too," followed by audience laughter.
Rachel and Ross look into each other's eyes, their hands still touching.
"Yeah, I got to get to the museum, so I'll see you tonight," Ross says, nodding slightly.
"Okay," Rachel says, keeping her gaze on him.
Ross leans forward and kisses Rachel. He then steps back, turns, and walks toward the front door.
"Oh," Rachel says, turning around to face the room. The audience vocalizes drawn-out "ooh" sounds.
Ross exits the apartment, closing the door behind him. Rachel faces Monica, Phoebe, Joey, and Chandler.
"Hi, guys," Rachel says, her lips parted in a wide smile.
Rachel walks past the television toward the kitchen area. Monica steps forward.
"Nice," an unidentified speaker says.
Joey and Chandler raise their hands toward Rachel and make loud, overlapping vocalizations. Audience laughter is heard.
"What's tonight?" Monica asks. "It is our first official date," Rachel says, smiling.
"Our first date," Rachel repeats, stopping near the kitchen counter.
Monica turns her head to look directly at Rachel, her smile fading. Mild audience laughter plays.
"Hello," Monica says, staring at Rachel.
"Hi," Rachel responds, looking back at Monica.
Monica steps toward Rachel. Phoebe sits nearby on the armchair, watching them.
"Tonight, you're supposed to waitress for me," Monica says, pointing an index finger at her own chest.
"My catering thing?" Monica continues, gesturing with her hands. "Any of those words trigger anything for you?" Rachel's eyes widen. "God," Rachel says, followed by audience laughter.
"Oh, God, Monica. I forgot. This is our first date," Rachel says, pressing her hands to her chest.
"Yes, but my mom got me this job, okay?" Monica says. Phoebe stands up and says, "I can be a waitress. I—"
"—can be a waitress," Phoebe finishes. Rachel grabs Phoebe's upper arms. "Oh, thank you, thank you, thank you, thank you," Rachel says rapidly. "Phoebe," Monica says.
"Phoebe," Monica repeats, looking at her.
"Really, Phoebe? Because you'd have to be an actual waitress," Monica says, gesturing toward Phoebe with open hands. Audience laughter is heard.
"This can't be like your I can be a bear cub thing," Monica adds. Audience laughter plays.
"I can be a waitress," Phoebe says. She steps forward and says, "Okay, watch this."
Phoebe raises one hand slightly and speaks quickly: "Give me two number ones. 86 the bacon one Adam and Eve on a raft and wreck them." Audience laughter is heard.
Phoebe lowers her hand and maintains her gaze on Monica.
An exterior shot shows a tall apartment building at night with illuminated windows. A siren sounds briefly.
Inside a kitchen, Richard, wearing a tuxedo, walks through a doorway. He checks his watch, walks past the counter, and approaches a closed door. Background party chatter is audible.
Richard opens the door. Monica and Phoebe stand in the hallway holding boxes and foil trays.
Phoebe smiles at Richard. Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe says, "James Bond."
Audience laughter continues. Richard smiles slightly.
Monica says, "Sorry we're late." Richard steps back, gesturing inward, and says, "That's okay. Come on in." The women step into the kitchen.
Richard closes the door and turns to them. "I'm sorry. Is Monica Geller coming? I was told she was," he says. Monica replies, "Dr."
Monica continues, "Burke? It's me, Monica." Richard stares at her.
Richard says, "My God, you used to be so I mean," moving his hands apart to indicate a wider shape. Audience laughter plays.
Monica smiles and looks down briefly.
Richard gestures toward her, saying, "you must have lost like"
Audience laughter plays as Monica smiles and shifts her weight.
Richard lowers his hands and says, "you look great."
Richard and Monica maintain eye contact as the audience laughter fades.
Monica says, "Thank you. This is my friend Phoebe. She's going to be helping me tonight." Richard extends his hand to Phoebe and says, "Hi, Phoebe. Nice to meet you." Phoebe smiles.
Phoebe shakes his hand while balancing her foil tray. Audience laughter plays. Monica sets her trays on the kitchen island.
Monica turns to Richard and asks, "So how you been?" her head tilted. Richard replies, "Great. Just great."
Monica asks, "Have you been?" maintaining her head tilt.
Richard says, "Oh, well, obviously, you know, Barbara and I split up."
Richard continues, "Otherwise you wouldn't have done the head tilt." Monica asks, "The head tilt?" Richard replies, "Yeah."
Richard tilts his head and demonstrates, saying, "Since the divorce, when anybody asks me how I am it's always with a sympathetic head tilt. How you doing? You okay?"
Monica straightens her posture, lowering her gaze slightly.
Monica says, "I'm sorry," her voice soft.
Richard raises his hands defensively. "No, no, it's fine. Believe me, I do it too," he says.
Richard continues, "I always answer with the I'm okay head bob," bobbing his head slightly.
Richard smiles and says, "I'm okay."
Monica smiles and looks at him.
Monica asks, "You sure?" Richard replies, "Yeah, I'm fine." He adds, "Hey, listen, I got to set up the music. I got a new CD changer."
Richard gestures with his hands, continuing, "Of course, the divorce only left me with four"
Richard continues, "CDs to change," and lowers his hands. Audience laughter plays.
Monica smiles and tilts her head again.
Monica says, "That's too bad." Richard smiles widely and says, "I'll survive."
The setting changes to an apartment living room. A basketball game plays on a television screen.

A sports commentator's voice says from the TV, "Warren for the reverse." Chandler and Joey sit side-by-side in black recliner chairs. Chandler holds a cordless phone to his ear and says, "Two larges, extra cheese on both."
Chandler raises his free hand.
Chandler says, "But listen, don't ring the buzzer for 19," gesturing with his raised hand.
Chandler continues, "Ring 20 geller green," pointing one finger up.
Chandler rests his hand on his chest and says, "They'll let you in. Okay."
Chandler lowers his hand and maintains his forward gaze.
Chandler speaks into the phone, "If you buz our door, there's no tip for you."
Chandler listens to the phone silently.
Chandler says, "Okay, thanks," and lowers the phone.
Chandler presses a button on the phone and places it on the table beside him. Joey remains still in his recliner.
Chandler says, "Pizza is on the way," keeping his eyes forward.
Joey replies, "I told you we wouldn't have to get up," looking toward the TV.
Both men sit motionless in their recliners, watching the screen.
Joey asks, "What if we have to pee?" his head resting against the chair back.
Chandler's eyes widen slightly. Audience laughter plays as the two men remain seated in silence.
Chandler reaches for the phone and says, "I'll cancel the sodas."
The setting changes to an exterior shot of a city skyline at night with many illuminated buildings.
The setting changes back to the kitchen. Monica stands at the island holding a cucumber, while Richard sits on a stool eating. Monica says, "You've got to get back out there. It's your party."
Richard points a skewer and says, "But they're so dull. They're all ophthalmologists." Monica replies, "You're an"
Monica finishes, "ophthalmologist." Richard says, "Only because my parents wanted me to be. I wanted to be a sheriff," holding up the skewer. Audience laughter plays.
Monica smiles and resumes preparing food.
Phoebe stands near the hallway door holding a glass. She says, "That's funny. Cadillac." A man in a tuxedo stands behind her in the hallway.
Phoebe says, "Cataract? I get it. No, I get it. If you stay out there,"
Phoebe finishes speaking and steps backward out of the kitchen.
Phoebe says, "you see." Monica leans forward and says, "All right, I tell you what." Richard nods.
Monica adds, "I'll come get you in five minutes with some sort of kebab emergency. Okay?" Richard replies, "Okay."
Richard stands up from the stool, adjusting his jacket.
Richard sighs, saying, "Better. Oh, God. Here we go," and walks toward the hallway door.
Richard stops near the door, turning back to Monica. He asks, "Hey, want to see him go nuts? Watch this."
Richard steps into the hallway, where party guests are visible.
Richard holds up a glass and says loudly, "Who needs glasses?"
Audience laughter is audible as the party guests look at Richard. Richard smiles widely.

In a kitchen, Monica stands near the counter wearing a black long-sleeve top under a white vest. Phoebe stands nearby wearing a white shirt under a black vest. Phoebe smiles as Monica turns slightly away.
Phoebe says, "You are so smitten." Monica turns back to her, smiling, and says, "I am not." Phoebe replies, "Oh, you are so much the smitten kitten." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica turns back to the counter, laughing softly.
Phoebe says, "You should ask him out. Dr."
Monica shakes her head while facing the counter.
Monica turns to Phoebe, gesturing with her hands, and says, "Burke? I don't think so. He's a grown up." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe smiles and shrugs.
Phoebe says, "So? You two are totally into each other." Monica gestures with both hands, saying, "Phoebe, he's a friend of my parents."
Monica looks at Phoebe, her expression unsmiling.
Monica continues, "He's like, 20 years older than me, okay?" Phoebe gestures forward with her hands and says, "So what, you're just never going to see him again?"
Monica looks down towards the counter.
Monica says, "Never." She picks up a small box, pauses, and adds, "I mean going to see him tomorrow at my eye appointment." Audience laughter is audible.
Phoebe watches Monica.
Phoebe asks, "You mean, like just get your eyes checked?"
Monica holds up the small box, saying, "Well, yeah, but you know, 27 is a dangerous eye age." Audience laughter is audible.
The hallway door opens, and Richard enters the kitchen wearing a tuxedo.
Richard looks back into the hallway and says, "They're picking teams to play. Guess my prescription." He then turns to Monica and adds, "Help me." Audience laughter is audible.
Monica smiles widely at Richard. Audience laughter continues.
The setting changes to a night view of city traffic, then changes to the exterior of an apartment door.

Ross and Rachel enter an apartment living room. Ross wears a plaid shirt under a black jacket. Rachel wears a dark brown long-sleeve top. Rachel says, "I'm not saying it was a bad movie. I'm just saying, you know, it was a little"
Rachel continues, "hard to follow." Ross closes the door and says, "I told you there were going to be subtitles."
Rachel turns away and says, "I know."
Rachel removes her jacket while Ross begins taking off his.
Rachel pushes her hair back and says, "I just didn't want to wear my glasses on our first date." Ross smiles at her.
Ross steps closer, taking Rachel's hands. They stand face-to-face, looking into each other's eyes.
Ross and Rachel lean in and kiss. Audience cheers are audible.
Ross keeps his eyes closed and mutters, "Monica," during the kiss.
Rachel pulls back quickly, her eyes wide as she looks at Ross. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross looks at her and says, "it would really help when I'm kissing if you didn't shout out my sister's name."
Rachel smiles slightly.
Rachel says, "I'm just checking," and steps past Ross, walking further into the room.
Ross walks forward, looking around the room.
Ross calls out, "Mon, Monica."
Ross continues, "Monica" as he looks around.
Ross stops walking, smiles slightly, and turns back toward Rachel.
Ross walks across the living room toward Rachel, who is standing near a lamp.
Rachel turns to face Ross as he approaches. They stand close together, looking into each other's eyes.
Ross places his hands on Rachel's waist. She wraps her arms around his neck, and they lean in to kiss.
Ross and Rachel continue kissing. Ross slides his hands further down Rachel's back.
Rachel suddenly pulls away, laughing, and covers her face with both hands. Ross looks at her and speaks briefly as audience laughter is audible.
Rachel lowers her hands, continuing to laugh, and speaks while placing her hand on her chest.
Rachel continues speaking, gesturing with her hands. Ross watches her with a slight smile.
Rachel says, "hands are on my butt." Audience laughter is audible.
Ross smiles, raising his eyebrows slightly.
Ross asks, "And that's funny, why?"
Rachel's smile fades as she looks at him.
Rachel says, "Well, it's not."
Rachel rests her hands near her waist.
Rachel says, "I'm sorry. I guess I'm just nervous. I mean, it's you, it's us. I mean, we're crossing"
Rachel continues, "that line. It's sort of a big thing." Ross replies, "Well, I know it's big. I just didn't know it was ha ha big." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks at Ross.
Rachel says, "Okay," stepping closer to him.
Ross and Rachel wrap their arms around each other and resume kissing.
They maintain their embrace, continuing to kiss until the segment ends.
In a living room, Ross stands looking toward Rachel, who is stepping back and laughing, holding her hands up. "my hands were nowhere near your butt," Ross says.
Rachel covers her face with one hand, laughing, and says, "No,"
Rachel steps backward, continuing to laugh.
Rachel lowers her hands and says, "I know. I know, I know. I was just thinking about when they were there the last time. I'm sorry." She raises her hands in an apologetic gesture.
Rachel continues to laugh, covering her mouth and waving her hand, while Ross stands still and watches her.
Rachel extends her hands forward, saying, "Okay, look. Whoa. I promise I'm good. I'm not going to laugh anymore."
Rachel gestures toward Ross and says, "Okay, put your hands back." Ross steps back, gesturing with his hands, and replies, "No, see, now I can't because"
Ross steps backward again, keeping his arms at his sides.
Ross says, "I'm feeling too self conscious." Rachel steps closer to him and says, "Come on, touch my butt." Ross replies, "No."
Rachel gestures toward herself.
Rachel leans forward, holding out one hand, and says, "Just one cheek."
Ross shakes his head and laughs.
Ross says, "The moment's gone. All right?" Rachel turns her back to him and says, "Just put your hands out, and I'll back up into it. That's romantic. Come on, touch it."
Ross says, "No." Rachel wiggles backward toward him, saying, "Oh, come on. Squeeze it." Ross steps back, saying, "No."
Rachel says, "Rub it." Ross says, "No." Rachel turns around to face him and asks, "Oh, come on. Would you just grab my ass?" Audience laughter is heard.
The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night; a streetlamp illuminates a fire escape.
The setting changes to a daytime view of a city skyline with a large bridge passing overhead.
Now in an apartment, Joey and Chandler sit in black leather recliners watching a television. On screen, a man sets fire to a car hood. A voice from the television says, "Quite simply."
Joey gestures toward the television and says, "Wow, look at that. The car is on fire yet somehow its expensive"
Joey keeps his gaze on the television screen.
Joey continues, "paint job is protected by the miracle."
Joey points at the television. Chandler turns his head to look at Joey as audience laughter sounds.
Chandler points to his own cheek and says to Joey, "Got a Cheeto on your face, man."
Joey touches his face, pulls a piece of food from his cheek, and puts it in his mouth.
Joey points at the television again, and both men continue watching. The sound of a door opening is audible.
Ross walks through the front door and says, "Hey."
Joey and Chandler turn their heads toward Ross. "Hey," they reply together.
Joey turns fully toward Ross with open hands and asks, "What are you doing here? Aren't you supposed to be out with Rachel?"
Ross stands near a table and looks downward.
Ross says, "That was 14 hours ago." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey turns his attention back toward the television.
Joey points at the television and says, "Whoa. We got to set the clock on this thing."
Ross walks over and sits on the arm of the sofa behind Joey.
Chandler says, "Side to go."
Ross looks toward Chandler and Joey, resting his hands in his lap.
Ross asks, "Listen, have you ever been fooling around with a girl and"
Joey and Chandler turn their heads to look at Ross.
Ross finishes, "started laughing?"
Joey and Chandler maintain their gaze on Ross.
Chandler nods and says, "Yeah, but it was 1982"
Chandler points near his face and adds, "and my Flock of Seagull's haircut was tickling her chin." Audience laughter is audible.
Joey turns toward Chandler briefly, then looks back at Ross.
Joey asks Ross, "She laughed at you?"
Ross nods and replies, "Yeah. It could have been worse. Could have been naked."
Joey and Chandler laugh and gesture toward Ross. Audience laughter continues.
Ross looks downward and says, "I don't know. I've been wanting this since, like, 9th grade typing,"
Ross continues, "and I just want it to be perfect and right." He shrugs slightly.
Joey turns back to the television, and Ross looks toward the screen.
Looking at the television, Joey asks, "Why isn't that laser beam cutting through the paint?"
Joey gestures toward the screen.
Ross replies, "It's the miracle."
Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "Certainly is a miracle."
The sound of a door opening is heard.
Rachel walks through the front door and says, "Hi, you guys."
Ross, Joey, and Chandler turn their heads toward her and reply, "Hey."
Rachel steps further inside.
Rachel says, "Hi."
Rachel leans over the counter next to Ross and speaks softly, "Listen, I was thinking about"
Chandler turns his head and gestures with his hands, saying, "listen, can you guys speak up? It's harder for us to hear you when you lower your voice." Audience laughter is audible.
Rachel looks closely at Ross, and Ross nods.
Ross says, "Hallway." Rachel replies, "Yeah."
Ross stands up, and he and Rachel walk to the door. Rachel exits into the hallway, with Ross following her.
In the hallway, Rachel turns to face Ross and says, "Okay."
Rachel touches Ross's jacket and says, "Listen, I'm sorry about last night, and I really want to make it up to you."
Ross looks at her and replies, "No, there's no need to make it up." Rachel asks, "How?"
Rachel continues holding his jacket lapels.
Rachel looks at Ross and says, "Well, I was thinking maybe a romantic dinner"
Rachel continues, "with candles and wine"
Rachel wraps her arms around Ross's neck and says, "and then maybe going back to."
The scene turns completely black, and silence falls.
The screen remains black until the segment ends.
